profile.title: Lotan
profile.introduction: Deals multiple instances of "Strike" damage in succession and summons a world-ending beast to devour enemies.

storySections.title: Basic Information
storySections.content:
Lotan has so many labels attached to her that no one knows how to approach this black-clad swordswoman.
Too humble, and she will shatter your knees for being boring; too arrogant, and she will slash your insolence with her greatsword; too tough, and she will soften your soul through battle.
But perhaps, this is the arrogance of the planet's first-born: as a former Guardian, she deserves her pride.

storySections.title: Story: I
storySections.content:
A.F. 180, as the Northern Hemisphere experiences a severe drought, the Korasuyu River sees its flow disrupted.
Local residents unearthed a monstrous skeleton from the riverbed's silt. Since the Korasuyu River signifies "chaos" in local culture, the skeleton was named the "Bone of Chaos."
Marine biologists identified it as the skeleton of a great winged whale, approximately 32 feet long and entirely covered in a silvery substance. The following year, this skeleton was auctioned at the Isis auction house for 130 million Leo pounds.
The trading firm refused to disclose the true identity of the buyer, but rumors suggest that the skeleton was transported to a private university on the outskirts of Londinium.

storySections.title: Story: II
storySections.content:
At Mythag University, the campus legend that "preparatory students must fight Lotan to become investigators" has intensified, pushing students' fear of the black-clad swordswoman to its peak. Many students specializing in theoretical research break out in cold sweats at the mere sight of her black skirt, afraid of locking eyes with the "battle maniac" and perishing on the training grounds.
Editor-in-chief Hannah Peasley conducted a brief special interview with the person concerned, during which Miss Lotan made the following statements:
"I don't have that much time to play house with babies."
"Soft-shelled shrimp do not deserve to be my opponents—are you one of them? Or do you have the courage to face Chaos?"

— As of the date of this publication, the editor-in-chief has never left the student dormitory. This edition of the "M University Newspaper" has been printed and distributed by intern editor Howard.

storySections.title: Story: III
storySections.content:
When it comes to choosing opponents, Lotan has her own standards.
Most scholarly warriors have read themselves into a corner, focusing solely on theoretical skills, which is rather annoying.
The elites in the army are often constrained by tedious formalities and moral codes, making them dull and uninspiring.
Only those rare talents, born with combat instincts and blessed by divine grace, can earn her favor.
The giant beasts, as ancient as the planet itself, suppress their battle desires and wait almost devoutly for their chosen opponents to reach their peak—what an extraordinarily gifted youth he is. He commands the power that originates from Aequor, and his fierce soul is capable of linking with the outworld Gods; given time, he will surely grow into a coveted powerhouse.
However, perhaps the passage of time has dulled her judgment; this time, the Spirit of Chaos has misjudged. For various reasons, Lotan does not wish to discuss this with anyone again.
All we know is that from then on, she sneers at anyone who shows "no ambition" or "no desire for progress."

storySections.title: Story: IV
storySections.content:
Born from Chaos, she embodies pure chaotic power, an untamed free soul, and an undefined order.
She craves challenges and battles; only by unleashing her overwhelming destructive urge can she fulfill her creator's sole wish.
—To safeguard the planet until the end, she has long yearned for a fierce Battle.
